page: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-48-lssd is an x86_64 architecture server powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 6985P-C processor. It features 48 vCPUs, derived from 24 physical cores with 2 threads per core, running at a base frequency of 2.3 GHz. The processor is equipped with a 504 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions. The system is configured with 180 GB of RAM, yielding 3.75 GB of memory per core, and includes 2951 GB of local NVMe SSD storage across 8 drives. No GPU hardware is present in this configuration.

Performance benchmarks show top-tier single-core capabilities, with Geekbench single-core and single-threaded gzip compression scores placing in the top 10% of tested servers. Multi-core performance is strong, with Geekbench multi-core, Passmark CPU, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression ranking in the top 25%, though stress-ng multi-core CPU performance falls into the average category. Memory bandwidth is strong for small cached blocks but drops to average for larger uncached blocks. The server demonstrates strong performance in database operations, Redis SET operations, and static web serving.

From a resource density perspective, the inclusion of nearly three terabytes of local NVMe SSD storage provides high local I/O capacity without the need for network-attached storage. However, the memory-to-vCPU ratio of 3.75 GB per core represents a tradeoff for memory-bound applications, which may exhaust the 180 GB RAM allocation before fully utilizing the 48 vCPUs. The hardware is optimized for compute and storage-intensive tasks that benefit from high single-core speed and local NVMe throughput rather than massive parallel memory bandwidth.

This server configuration is suited for database hosting, software compilation, image processing, and static web serving. It is also capable of handling small-to-medium scale LLM inference and text processing workloads that leverage Intel Xeon matrix acceleration and high-speed local storage.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-48-lssd is an x86_64 server featuring 48 vCPUs from a dedicated Intel Xeon 6985P-C processor running at 2.3 GHz, paired with 180 GB of RAM and 2951 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. The architecture includes advanced vector and matrix extensions (AVX-512 and AMX) and a 504 MB L3 cache. Benchmarks show top-tier single-core CPU and image processing performance, alongside strong multi-core execution, database operations, and static web serving. While memory bandwidth for large uncached blocks and multi-core stress-ng performance remain average, the server delivers high local storage throughput and strong small-to-medium LLM inference speeds. This configuration is suited for database management, software compilation, web serving, and local-storage-intensive workloads.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-48-lssd is an x86_64 server with 48 vCPUs, 180 GB RAM, and 2951 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, delivering top-tier single-core performance and strong database capabilities.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-48-lssd features 48 vCPUs, 180 GB RAM, and 2951 GB local NVMe SSD storage for database and compute workloads.
tagline: An x86_64 platform combining forty-eight dedicated vCPUs with nearly three terabytes of local NVMe storage for database and compute workloads.
bullet_points: 48 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 6985P-C processor
180 GB of system memory paired with 2951 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier single-core CPU, image processing, and compression performance
Strong database operations, Redis throughput, and static web serving
Well-suited for database hosting, software compilation, and medium LLM inference
